intro: The clearest split between BoxyHQ and Turnkey is how they charge: BoxyHQ uses subscription pricing starting around $770, while Turnkey uses usage-based pricing. BoxyHQ specializes in Enterprise SSO; it fits best for Apps, B2B. Turnkey is Wallet infrastructure and key management API using secure enclaves for crypto and fintech apps; it fits best for Fintech and crypto infra teams building custom wallet UX on top. Pick BoxyHQ when the job maps to apps needing sso/mfa; pick Turnkey when it maps to secure enclave key management api for crypto fintech apps. BoxyHQ is not for internal, static projects. Turnkey is not for product teams wanting a ready-made end-user wallet sdk. Honest trade-offs: BoxyHQ is weak for internal tools with basic auth, and Turnkey is weak for primitive-heavy; you build ux on top yourself — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the Identity / Auth / User Management umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate.
quick_take: BoxyHQ is for apps, b2b; Turnkey is for fintech and crypto infra teams building custom wallet ux on ; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose BoxyHQ if you want subscription pricing starting around $770 and your workload matches apps, b2b.
verdict_b: Choose Turnkey if you want usage-based pricing and your workload matches fintech and crypto infra teams building custom wallet ux on top.

faq.a: A direct comparison depends on your usage profile and which pricing model fits better. BoxyHQ uses subscription pricing starting at $770. Turnkey uses usage-based pricing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from BoxyHQ to Turnkey?
faq.a: Migration from BoxyHQ (low lock-in) to Turnkey (medium lock-in) is the harder direction; the reverse is easier. Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

BoxyHQ scores higher on developer experience (5/5 vs 4/5 for Turnkey). Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is Turnkey a good alternative to BoxyHQ?
faq.a: Turnkey sits in the same category as BoxyHQ, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. Turnkey is best fit for fintech and crypto infra teams building custom wallet ux on top. Note: Turnkey is not for product teams wanting a ready-made end-user wallet sdk.
